message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts (with src/cli.ts wrapper)
Failing test: "claim damage entries exceed insured count of that type causes non-zero exit"
Current error: exitCode is 0 — no validation that damage entries don't exceed insured count
Passing tests so far: 27

The test has a policy with 1 sword and a claim with 2 sword damages. Expected: runCli returns non-zero exitCode with stderr matching /exceed|too many|sword/i.

Per the kata spec: "if the damages array contains more entries of a given type than the policy actually covers (e.g. two sword damages but only one sword insured) → the CLI exits with a non-zero status code; the whole claim is rejected"

Add validation: count damages by itemType, count policy items by type, throw an Error if any damage-count exceeds the corresponding policy-count.

Existing test "claim with multiple damaged items: deductible applies per damage entry" has 2 items (1 sword + 1 amulet) and 2 damages (1 each) — that must still pass.

Keep all 27 existing tests passing. Minimal change only.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return when done.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal validation needed.
Now I'll add minimal validation to count damages by type and verify they don't exceed policy counts.

content.text:
All 28 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:

**Implementation**: Added `countByType` helper and `validateDamageCounts` function that counts damages by `itemType` and policy items by `type`, throwing an error when any damage-count exceeds the corresponding policy-count.

**Result**: All 28 tests now pass (was 27, now includes the new "claim damage entries exceed insured count" test).

**Approach**: Minimal addition — a small generic counting helper and a count-comparison validation hooked into `validateDamagesAgainstPolicy`. The error message includes the item type (e.g., "too many sword damages: exceed insured count"), satisfying the `/exceed|too many|sword/i` regex. The existing multi-item test (1 sword + 1 amulet, with 1 damage each) still passes because each damage count equals its policy count.

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_23-33-33_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
